latest.title: Crossroads Well Southern Oklahoma USA Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6920C
Union Jack Oil PLC
01 May 2026
1 May 2026
Union Jack Oil plc
("Union Jack" or the "Company")
Crossroads Well Southern Oklahoma USA Update
Union Jack Oil plc (AIM: UJO), a UK and USA focused onshore hydrocarbon, production, development, exploration and investment company, announces that, further to the update on 16 April 2026,
Reach Oil and Gas Company Inc. (the "Operator"), has informed the Company that the rig at Crossroads Well, located in Southern Oklahoma is still undergoing maintenance and repair. A spud date
will be provided in early May
.
Union Jack currently holds a 43% interest in the project. The Company's share of the drilling costs have already been funded from its existing cash resources.
For further information, please contact:

Crossroads Well Southern Oklahoma USA Update
Union Jack Oil plc (AIM: UJO), a UK and USA focused onshore hydrocarbon, production, development, exploration and investment company, announces that, following the update on 8 April 2026, Reach Oil and Gas Company Inc. (the "Operator"), has informed the Company that there has been a short delay to the spud date for the Crossroads well, located in Southern Oklahoma, USA as a result of rig maintenance requirements. The Operator is confident that the well will be spudded before the end of April 2026.
Union Jack currently holds a 43% interest. The Company's share of the drilling costs have already been funded from the Company's existing cash resources.

latest.title: Crossroads Well Southern Oklahoma USA Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5324Z
Union Jack Oil PLC
08 April 2026
8 April 2026
Union Jack Oil plc
("Union Jack" or the "Company")
Crossroads Well Southern Oklahoma USA Update
Union Jack Oil plc (AIM: UJO), a UK and USA focused onshore hydrocarbon, production, development, exploration and investment company, is pleased to announce that Reach Oil and Gas Company Inc. (the "Operator"), has informed the Company that the Crossroads well, located in Southern Oklahoma, USA will be spudded on or around 16 April 2026. Union Jack currently holds a 43% interest. The Company's share of the drilling costs have already been funded.
·
Crossroads will penetrate a large, 100 acre structure in the prolific Oil Creek Sand
·
Estimated recoverable resource by the Operator over multiple target zones of 1,670,000 barrels of oil gross
·
Crossroads is a large four-way dip closed structure mapped on 3D seismic

latest.title: West Newton Permit Variation
latest.content:
Today 11:39
RNS Number : 1897T
Union Jack Oil PLC
16 February 2026
16 February 2026
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
Union Jack Oil plc
("Union Jack" or the "Company")
West Newton Permit Variation
Union Jack Oil plc (AIM: UJO, OTCQB: UJOGF), a UK and USA focused onshore hydrocarbon, production, development, exploration and investment company notes today's announcement from Rathlin Energy U.K. Limited ("Rathlin") regarding the West Newton gas development project, located within PEDL183, onshore UK in East Yorkshire.
Rathlin, operator of the licence, has been informed by the Environment Agency ("EA") that it has issued Rathlin the variation to the permit for the West Newton 'A' Well Site to include a reservoir stimulation in the WNA-2 well, subject to certain pre-operational conditions. The recompletion works are designed to overcome well bore damage that was sustained during earlier drilling operations.
The Company believes that the planned recompletion of the WNA-2 well is a low-cost and low risk means of further derisking the sub-surface element of the West Newton project and gathering data to optimise future production wells.
This key regulatory milestone clears a major hurdle in the path toward development and eventual production at West Newton, strengthening Rathlin's, and consequently Union Jack's, ability to advance its UK onshore assets within a responsible environmental and social governance framework.
The Company is confident that West Newton will prove to be an important strategic asset to the UK as the country looks to secure domestic energy supply and affordable energy.
Union Jack holds a 16.665% interest in PEDL183.
Rathlin's full announcement can be viewed here:
https://www.rathlin-energy.co.uk/latest-update/
For further information, please contact:
Union Jack Oil plc
info@unionjackoil.com
David Bramhill
SP Angel Corporate Finance LLP

company_30s.what_they_do: UJO Ltd is a small oil‑and‑gas exploration and production company that owns and develops a handful of fields in the UK and the US.  Its core assets include the Wressle, Keddington and Moccasin wells, and the West Newton and Sark projects, where the firm is working to bring new wells online and produce gas and oil.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company taps into the continuing demand for energy while also exploring battery‑related opportunities.  Its assets sit in regions with growing infrastructure support and a regulatory environment that is gradually easing, giving UJO a niche advantage in a fragmented market where larger players dominate.
company_30s.current_state: UJO is still in the early production phase, relying on farm‑in deals and permit approvals to keep its wells running.  Cash burn is moderate, but the share price is trading close to a historic low after a sharp sell‑off.

rns_narrative: Permit and production updates – the company has been filing variations for the West Newton permit, resuming production at Keddington, and announcing a new farm‑in for the Sark well.
Governance and funding moves – director changes, a £2 million subscription for growth, and share purchases by a non‑executive director signal ongoing capital activity.
Regulatory and planning activity – recent planning updates and approvals suggest the firm is actively clearing the bottlenecks that have kept its projects stalled.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent but hopeful – investors are quietly waiting for the next drill‑out or funding announcement.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls focus on UJO’s three solid producing assets and its potential battery‑related diversification, seeing the current low as a buying opportunity.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears warn that permit delays, limited cash and a volatile oil market could keep the price stuck or lower; they fear the company may struggle to keep wells running.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has been flat, but recent posts hint at optimism around the West Newton and Sark developments, suggesting a possible shift if those projects move forward.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no recent spikes.  A quiet backdrop means any news or announcement could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Final approval of the West Newton permit variation or a clear production milestone from that well.
First production from the Keddington field or the Sark well, proving the company can turn its assets into cash.
Completion of the £2 million subscription or a new farm‑in deal that injects fresh capital.
A battery‑related partnership announcement that could diversify the company’s revenue base.
A rally in oil and gas prices that lifts the valuation of all small producers.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sharp rise in trading volume around any of the above announcements, a break above the 200‑day moving average, or a sudden spike in positive tweets from key analysts.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock sits in the early stage of a deep crash.  With a tight compression on the chart, the next bounce will likely be triggered once permits are cleared and production starts, giving a window of roughly 3–6 months for a reversal.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Deep crashes historically deliver strong rebounds – the signal framework shows that a 70‑95% drop can yield 160% average returns.  If UJO clears its permits and starts production, the price could climb to roughly half of its all‑time high, offering a 2.5‑fold upside from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If permits keep getting delayed, production stalls, or the oil market stays weak, the price could remain flat or fall further, leaving the company in a cash‑burn situation.  The worst case is a continued decline to a new low, erasing any upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk is limited to the current level; upside could be about 2.5× if the price climbs halfway to its all‑time high.  The reward‑to‑risk ratio is roughly 2.5:1, making this an attractive asymmetrical play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the share breaks above its 200‑day moving average with a volume spike of at least 30% over the 5‑day average.  Use a position size that keeps a potential loss below 10% of capital, and place a stop just below the recent low to guard against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the West Newton permit approval or a production announcement.  If the share rises 15% from the recent low within 48 hours of the news, consider a partial entry.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – no delisting, bankruptcy or fraud risk identified.

ai_insights.bottom_line: UJO is a deep‑crashed, low‑sentiment oil & gas play that could rebound sharply if permits and production come through, offering a 2.5‑fold upside for a modest downside.
